scope.principle: Each capability receives an individual verdict while coherent scientific DAGs share data loading, dependencies, and reference artifacts.

scenarios.title: Dataset intake and experimental-design identifiability
scientific_questions: Can the runtime distinguish counts, normalized expression, and transformed layers?
Can it identify controls, perturbations, independent units, MOI, and guide design without guessing?
Does it block analyses when replicate structure or estimands are not identifiable?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Materialize the declared matrix and identities without changing cell or gene identity.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block nonnumeric, misaligned, duplicated, or over-budget inputs before scientific analysis.
capabilities.reference: Source H5AD dimensions, identity hashes, layer metadata, and sparse/dense invariants.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Report confirmed, inferred, and unresolved contract fields separately.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Never promote inferred design facts to confirmed facts.
capabilities.reference: Frozen design-confirmation records and planted unresolved fields.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Detect matrix, identity, missingness, and layer-integrity problems.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block planted structural corruption and report the affected artifact.
capabilities.reference: Source artifact profile plus planted corruption fixtures.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Summarize perturbation, control, replicate, donor, and batch balance at the correct unit.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block or caution on confounding, missing replicates, and unsupported estimands.
capabilities.reference: Direct tabulations from frozen cell-selection metadata.

scenarios.title: Guide identity, assignment, and ambient contamination
scientific_questions: Are RNA and guide barcodes aligned?
Are guide-to-target mappings complete and nonconflicting?
Can true assignments be separated from ambient guide counts and uncertain cells?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Audit barcode alignment, assignment coverage, multiplicity, and guide-target consistency.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Report label-only assignment as label-only and block raw-count claims when guide counts are absent.
capabilities.reference: Papalexi exported guide matrix, guide map, RNA barcodes, and metadata assignments.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Verify guide feature, barcode, target-map, and matrix identity consistency.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block mismatched or duplicate guide assets.
capabilities.reference: Checksummed Papalexi guide assets and planted alignment attacks.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Estimate guide-specific ambient contamination without relabeling uncertain cells as measured assignments.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Detect planted ambient-heavy guides and retain uncertainty.
capabilities.reference: Planted guide-count mixtures calibrated to Papalexi sparsity and count depth.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Recover positive assignments and posterior uncertainty from sparse guide counts.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block when only labels are available or when the guide matrix exceeds resource limits.
capabilities.reference: Planted negative-binomial mixtures plus Papalexi metadata concordance as a real-data proxy.

scenarios.title: MOI, multi-guide cells, doublets, and retained-cell policy
scientific_questions: Does the runtime distinguish intended guide combinations from cell doublets?
Does the retained-cell policy follow confirmed MOI and guide design?
Do downstream analyses actually consume the retained-cell manifest?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Separate guide multiplicity, intended combinations, and transcriptomic doublets under confirmed design facts.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Never label Norman intended combinations as doublets solely because multiple guides are present.
capabilities.reference: Norman construct identities, Papalexi multiplicity metadata, and planted transcriptomic doublets.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Emit deterministic retained and excluded cell identities with explicit reasons.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block zero-row selections and stale, wrong-scope, or missing upstream QC.
capabilities.reference: Frozen selection metadata and direct rule application.

scenarios.title: Control-state reference, mapping, and candidate annotation
scientific_questions: Is the reference fitted only on eligible control cells?
Are out-of-reference evaluation cells rejected rather than forcibly mapped?
Are inferred state labels kept at candidate authority?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Fit PCA and Leiden only on split-scoped controls and record seeds and features.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block noncontrol or evaluation leakage.
capabilities.reference: Independent Scanpy/Seurat control-only reference and input identity audit.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Produce a frozen sparse feature/PCA/kNN reference under the memory budget.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block full-matrix densification and insufficient controls before allocation.
capabilities.reference: Independent control-only representation and planted sparse resource fixture.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Map retained evaluation cells in chunks and reject out-of-reference states.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block missing retained-cell dependencies and avoid forced nearest-state assignment.
capabilities.reference: Planted held-out states plus Papalexi evaluation mapping.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Generate evidence-linked candidate state annotations.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Keep ambiguous or unsupported labels unresolved and nonstrong.
capabilities.reference: Planted marker sets and candidate-authority policy.

scenarios.title: Perturbation efficacy, responder heterogeneity, and target reliability
scientific_questions: Is efficacy supported by direct expression, a response signature, or both?
Are responder, escape, guide heterogeneity, and replicate overlap represented per target?
Are target-level results joined by stable target identity rather than dataset-level shortcuts?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Estimate direct and signature efficacy with design-correct direction and uncertainty.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Keep absent targets and signature-only evidence unresolved.
capabilities.reference: Direct target-expression contrasts and frozen signature references.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Classify per-target responder and escape cells from the frozen control representation.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block missing state representations and never reuse another target's classification.
capabilities.reference: Frozen Seurat/Pertpy Mixscape reference on Papalexi.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Remain compatible for frozen v0.2 behavior without becoming the primary scientific path.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Never bypass the aggregate target-identity and dependency rules.
capabilities.reference: Compatibility snapshot and authority-policy regression.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Join guide efficacy, responder evidence, QC, and replicate support per target_uid.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Mark missing target-specific inputs unresolved rather than borrowing dataset-level evidence.
capabilities.reference: Direct per-target joins from frozen upstream artifacts.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Report pooled, unpooled, leave-one-guide-out, and direction-consistency sensitivity per target.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Expose rather than hide guide-specific reversals and unstable targets.
capabilities.reference: Independent guide-level recomputation from frozen effect tables.

scenarios.title: Effect estimation, independent replication, and null calibration
scientific_questions: Is the independent experimental unit used rather than the cell?
Is method selection compatible with MOI, guide data, and the estimand?
Are effect, composition, and null-calibration claims kept distinct?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Fit paired or unpaired pseudobulk edgeR at the donor/replicate unit.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block confounding and caution on insufficient independent units.
capabilities.reference: Independent direct-R edgeR execution on Kang.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Control type-I error and recover planted high-MOI guide-gene associations.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Correctly block Norman when cell-by-guide counts are unavailable.
capabilities.reference: Synthetic high-MOI truth plus Norman suitability refusal.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Estimate donor-level cell-state composition effects.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block cell-level pseudoreplication and confounded designs.
capabilities.reference: Independent direct-R Propeller execution on Kang.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Quantify null behavior for the selected statistical method.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Never label missing or uncomputed null metrics as passed.
capabilities.reference: Planted null permutations with frozen seeds.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Assess replicate-aware null contrasts without breaking the design structure.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject permutations that exchange nonexchangeable donors or batches.
capabilities.reference: Design-preserving planted replicate-null fixtures.

scenarios.title: Effect matrices and module discovery
scientific_questions: Can target-level effects be assembled without silently filling missing or blocked results?
Are imported and learned modules evaluated with the correct gene universe?
Are learned modules stable across rank and seed rather than treated as unique truth?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Assemble target-by-feature effects with explicit missingness and source identities.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Preserve blocked and missing targets instead of zero imputation.
capabilities.reference: Independent join of frozen target-effect artifacts.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Aggregate signed feature effects into module-level target effects.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Report insufficient module coverage and preserve effect direction.
capabilities.reference: Independent weighted module aggregation.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Import deterministic gene sets with provenance and overlap statistics.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject malformed, empty, duplicate, or wrong-role GMT assets.
capabilities.reference: Frozen GMT parser reference and planted malformed files.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Recover planted sparse modules across multiple ranks and seeds.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block over-budget densification and expose unstable solutions.
capabilities.reference: Planted modules calibrated to real sparse matrices.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Learn stable modules only from retained split-scoped controls.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Detect perturbed/evaluation-cell leakage and unconsumed control dependencies.
capabilities.reference: Planted control programs and control-cell identity audit.

scenarios.title: Response programs, regulators, pathways, and perturbation networks
scientific_questions: Are positive and negative response directions preserved?
Are perturbation clusters stable and supported by effect data?
Are regulator and pathway priors kept separate from measured effects?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Recover planted signed response programs while preserving direction.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Expose sign ambiguity and unstable components.
capabilities.reference: Planted signed target-by-gene programs.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Cluster perturbations from frozen effects with bootstrap stability.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Keep unstable assignments unresolved.
capabilities.reference: Planted perturbation groups plus real-data stability analysis.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Recover signed regulator activity from effect profiles and a frozen network.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Keep prior network support distinct from measured activity.
capabilities.reference: Planted regulator activities and frozen network snapshot.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Construct evidence-linked perturbation-regulator candidate edges.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Never render candidate or prior-supported edges as causal measurements.
capabilities.reference: Planted edge provenance and claim-role policy.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Compute ORA with the declared tested-gene universe and multiple-testing correction.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block missing universes and report low-overlap modules.
capabilities.reference: Independent hypergeometric calculation on frozen GMT.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Evaluate full signed rankings against frozen gene sets.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject truncated significant-only lists and duplicate ranks.
capabilities.reference: Independent preranked GSEA implementation with frozen seeds.

scenarios.title: Evidence synthesis, literature context, and next-panel design
scientific_questions: Are measured results, predictions, priors, and hypotheses kept separate?
Are contradictions and missing target-specific evidence visible?
Does next-panel design prioritize uncertainty and coverage rather than effect size alone?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Return reproducible, cited literature priors from a frozen response snapshot.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Never convert literature support into a measured dataset result.
capabilities.reference: Frozen Europe PMC query/response fixtures.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Map findings to measured, prediction, prior, and hypothesis sources with contradictions.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject stale, unsupported, or cross-target evidence for strong claims.
capabilities.reference: Planted mixed-source evidence graph.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Select a constrained panel balancing uncertainty, coverage, guide support, and feasibility.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Do not rank solely by effect size or use unsupported strong evidence.
capabilities.reference: Planted constrained-selection problems with known feasible optima or Pareto sets.

scenarios.title: Virtual perturbation prediction ingestion, leakage, and evaluation
scientific_questions: Are training, calibration, and evaluation targets and cells separated?
Are prediction identities, directions, uncertainty, and baselines validated before scoring?
Are predictions prevented from becoming measured scientific claims?

capabilities.positive_expectation: Bind disjoint target, construct, and cell identities to virtual-model splits.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject any planted train/evaluation overlap.
capabilities.reference: Frozen Norman split manifest and planted overlap attacks.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Ingest chunked predictions with stable cell, target, gene, direction, and uncertainty identities.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject missing, duplicate, transposed, drifted, or over-budget prediction bundles.
capabilities.reference: Controlled Norman prediction bundles with planted identity attacks.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Detect intentionally leaky predictions and certify only clean split contact.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Block scoring after any forbidden test contact.
capabilities.reference: Clean and intentionally leaky controlled prediction bundles.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Generate deterministic no-change and training-mean baselines without evaluation leakage.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Reject baselines that use evaluation outcomes.
capabilities.reference: Independent blockwise baseline calculations.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Score effect magnitude, direction, and rank against held-out observed effects.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Keep model outputs in the prediction source class.
capabilities.reference: Independent blockwise evaluator on controlled Norman predictions.

capabilities.positive_expectation: Add uncertainty calibration, bootstrap intervals, discriminability, and baseline comparisons.
capabilities.negative_expectation: Report missing uncertainty or references as unavailable rather than passed.
capabilities.reference: Controlled predictions with known uncertainty and discriminability structure.
